single-file-edit.description: Update a single script and rerun quick tests
single-file-edit.prompt: In shell/common.sh, document why run_cmd enforces strict shell flags and keep behavior unchanged.
single-file-edit.success: Guard passes and make quick succeeds.

multi-file-refactor.description: Coordinated refactor across two operator scripts
multi-file-refactor.prompt: Update browser_operator_open_app.sh and browser_operator_open_and_click.sh to share a helper function for retries, keeping behavior identical otherwise.
validations:
python3 - <<'PY'
import re, subprocess, sys
files = [
    'browser_operator_open_app.sh',
    'browser_operator_open_and_click.sh'
]
cmd = ['git', 'diff', '--'] + files
try:
    diff = subprocess.check_output(cmd).decode()
except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
    sys.stderr.write(f'ERROR: unable to read diff: {exc}\n')
    sys.exit(1)
helper_candidates = set()
current = None
defined = {}
for line in diff.splitlines():
    if line.startswith('+++ '):
        path = line[4:].strip()
        if path.startswith('b/'):
            path = path[2:]
        current = path if path in files else None
        continue
    if current is None or not line.startswith('+') or line.startswith('+++'):
        continue
    content = line[1:].strip()
    if not content:
        continue
    m = re.search(r'(\w*(?:helper|retry)\w*)', content)
    if m:
        helper = m.group(1)
        helper_candidates.add(helper)
        defined.setdefault(helper, set()).add(current)
if not helper_candidates:
    sys.stderr.write('ERROR: no helper-like additions detected\n')
    sys.exit(1)
missing = [h for h, files_used in defined.items() if len(files_used) < 2]
if len(missing) == len(helper_candidates):
    sys.stderr.write('ERROR: no helper identifier used in both files\n')
    sys.exit(1)
print('helper-intent validation ok')
PY
multi-file-refactor.success: Guard passes, helper extracted, quick tests succeed.

debug-failing-test.prompt: Tests report a login regression; inspect browser_operator_login_flow.sh and tests/mock_login_flow.sh to address the failing case described in the console output.

doc-code-alignment.success: Guard passes and make check succeeds.

guard-edge-case.description: Stress guard limits with near-cap scope
guard-edge-case.prompt: Touch at most three docs (docs/codex-operator-workflow.md, docs/aider-performance-guide.md, docs/aider-summary.md) to add a short section on benchmarking without exceeding guard limits.
validations:
python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess, sys
allowed = [
    'docs/codex-operator-workflow.md',
    'docs/aider-performance-guide.md',
    'docs/aider-summary.md'
]
cmd = ['git', 'diff', '--'] + allowed
try:
    diff = subprocess.check_output(cmd).decode()
except subprocess.CalledProcessError as exc:
    sys.stderr.write(f'ERROR: unable to read diff: {exc}\n')
    sys.exit(1)
for line in diff.splitlines():
    if line.startswith('+') and not line.startswith('+++'):
        content = line[1:].strip()
        if content and 'benchmark' in content.lower():
            print('benchmark-content validation ok')
            sys.exit(0)
sys.stderr.write('ERROR: no added line mentioning benchmark found\n')
sys.exit(1)
PY
guard-edge-case.success: Guard accepts the limited scope without violations.
